reasonc
z/ True if we are running on a Power PC platform.Z
powerpcZ
ppc)
platformZ	processor
machine
startswithr
on_powerpc#
The blocklisted trig functions are not accurate on aarch64 for
    complex256. Rather than dig through the actual problem skip the
    test. This should be fixed when we can move past glibc2.17
    which is the version in manylinux2014
    g
-(;)
arcsinhZ
float128Z
complex256
real
abs)
bad_arcsinh)
aarch64z
Trig functions of np.longcomplex values known to be inaccurate on aarch64 for some compilation configurations, should be fixed by building on a platform using glibc>2.17

TestFrompyfuncc
z)TestFrompyfunc.test_identity.<locals>.mulrF
Z	mul_ufuncr
z.TestFrompyfunc.test_identity.<locals>.<lambda>)
TestFrompyfunc.test_identityN)
    Check for a branch cut in a function.
    Assert that `x0` lies on a branch cut of function `f` and `f` is
    continuous from the direction `dx`.
    Parameters
    ----------
    f : func
        Function to check
    x0 : array-like
        Point on branch cut
    dx : array-like
        Direction to check continuity in
    re_sign, im_sign : {1, -1}
        Change of sign of the real or imaginary part expected
    sig_zero_ok : bool
        Whether to check if the branch cut respects signed zero (if applicable)
    dtype : dtype
        Dtype to check (should be complex)
    
@g-C
atleast_1dr
re_signZ
im_signZ
sig_zero_okrM
scalerY
jir+
